articles.title: TanStack Hotkeys: A Type-Safe, Cross-Platform Hotkey Library
articles.description: The TanStack family gains a new member: Hotkeys, an alpha-stage universal keyboard interaction toolkit. It handles cross-environment quirks, supports multi-step sequences (like Vim commands or cheat codes), and can record user shortcuts. Here's the full pitch.
articles.author: TanStack LLC

articles.title: Announcing TypeScript 6.0 Beta
articles.description: v6.0 is largely a “time to clean up your tsconfig” release, designed to bridge the transition to the Go-powered native TypeScript 7 later this year. Be aware of some tweaks, like types defaulting to [] and --strict now being true by default, among many more breaking changes and deprecations.
articles.author: Microsoft

articles.title: Experiments with CodeMirror
articles.description: CodeMirror is one of the most robust code editor components out there (we’ve just used it while rebuilding our newsletter editor!) and it’s very extensible too, as seen in this walkthrough of building a VSCode-like ‘change review’ feature for it.
articles.author: Antonio De Lucreziis

articles.title: How to Make an HTTP Request in Node.js
articles.description: A comprehensive guide to using fetch in production, tackling timeouts, streaming requests and responses, retries, concurrency, mocking, and more. Most of this is useful in the broader JavaScript context.
articles.author: Luciano Mammino

articles.title: Implementing Virtual Scrolling at Billion-Row Scale
articles.description: A walkthrough of tackling the numerous challenges involved in building a table component (HighTable) that handles billions of rows.
articles.author: Sylvain Lesage

articles.title: JS-Heavy Approaches Aren't Compatible with Long-Term Performance Goals
articles.description: A web performance engineer at Automattic makes the case against JS-heavy architectures and in support of a more server-centric approach.
articles.author: Sérgio Gomes

articles.title: Electrobun v1: A Bun-Based Desktop App Approach
articles.description: An introduction to a cross-platform runtime for JS/TS desktop apps. It uses the OS’s default web renderer (like Neutralinojs) with Bun as the engine and bundler behind the scenes. The resulting bundles are as small as 12MB. (Homepage and GitHub repo.)
articles.author: Blackboard

articles.title: fetch-network-simulator: Intercept fetch to Simulate Poor Network Conditions
articles.description: The heart of this is a library that intercepts fetch and applies rules to do things like drop random requests, delay them, or slow them down, so you can see how resilient your app is.
articles.author: Karn Pratap Singh

articles.title: Peek: A Lightweight Library for Smart Page Header Behavior
articles.description: You can see it in action on the page. It’s “smart” in that it will differentiate between small insignificant scrolls and intentional ones. You can customize the thresholds and delays and it works with any framework. Repo here.
articles.author: Chad Pierce

articles.title: 🏎️ Rari: Runtime-Accelerated Rendering Infrastructure
articles.description: A React Server Components framework but with a Rust-powered server runtime, aiming for higher throughput and lower latency. The getting started guide will help you get the idea.
articles.author: Ryan Skinner

articles.title: Broad Infinite List: A Bidirectional Infinite List for React and Vue
articles.description: Smoothly stream logs, feed updates, or chat history in both directions without layout shifts.
articles.author: suhaotian

articles.description: 📊 Hot on the heels of the recent State of JS survey results come the State of React results.
🤖 Google shows off a preview of WebMCP, an attempt to create a standard way for web sites to offer up abilities for AI agents to use.
Biome 2.4 – The formatting/linting tool can now handle embedded CSS & GraphQL in JS files, improves Vue and Svelte support, and adds HTML accessibility linting rules.
React Native 0.84 – Hermes V1 is now the default JS engine on iOS and Android, iOS ships with precompiled binaries, and React 19.2.3.
Node.js 25.6.1 (Current) and Node.js 24.13.1 (LTS)
sql.js v1.14.0 – SQLite compiled to JavaScript.

articles.title: Announcing Interop 2026
articles.description: The Interop project forms the basis for Google, Apple, Igalia, Microsoft, and Mozilla to collaborate each year on implementing the web technology that developers most want and need. This year sees fifteen brand new topics, including improvements to attr(), container style queries, contrast-color(), scroll-driven animations, and tightening up the CSS Scroll Snap spec.
articles.author: Wu and Simmons (WebKit)

articles.title: Modern CSS Code Snippets
articles.description: It has a few rough edges, but the core idea of showing a variety of CSS snippets with a before/after showing the “old” way to do something versus the “new” way is neat. I picked up a few quick techniques I wasn’t aware of.
articles.author: Naeem Noor

articles.title: Implementing Virtual Scrolling at Billion-Row Scale
articles.description: A technical walkthrough of handling DOM limits, scrollbar precision problems, and accessibility constraints in building a table component (HighTable) that handles billions of rows efficiently.
articles.author: Sylvain Lesage

articles.title: Approximating contrast-color() with Other CSS Features
articles.description: With contrast-color() not yet widely supported, this article derives a one-line CSS formula using oklch() relative color syntax to automatically choose black or white text against any background.
articles.author: Kevin Hamer (CSS Tricks)

articles.title: Gwtar: A Static, Efficient, Single-File HTML Format
articles.description: A technical explanation of a new polyglot HTML archival format that provides a single, self-contained, HTML file but which can be efficiently lazy-loaded by a web browser through the use of some cute techniques.
articles.author: Gwern

articles.title: Justifying text-wrap: pretty
articles.description: “Although Safari is the first browser to ship a non-joke implementation of text-wrap, the combination with text-align looks ugly..”
articles.author: Alex Kladov

articles.title: Oat: Ultra-Light, No-Dependency HTML UI Component Library
articles.description: This one got a lot of love on social media this week! Oat is built on the idea of just throwing a CSS and JS file into your project (totalling about 8KB gzipped) and getting a complete UI component library at your disposal with a shadcn-inspired aesthetic and without any arduous build steps.
articles.author: Kailash Nadh

articles.title: 🕒  Slowmo: Slow Down, Pause, or Speed Up Browser Time
articles.description: Not quite a DeLorean, but a tool (usable in your own code or as an extension) that slows down time in the browser for debugging animations, studying demos, and so on. It slows down a variety of things like CSS animations, transitions, and requestAnimationFrame.
articles.author: Francois Laberge

articles.title: ✉️  Maizzle: Build HTML Emails with Tailwind CSS
articles.description: A framework that helps you quickly build HTML emails with Tailwind CSS and a toolchain to apply advanced email optimizations and customizations (like inlining CSS, rendering Markdown, and creating plain text versions).
articles.author: Maizzle

articles.title: TanStack Hotkeys: A Type-Safe, Cross-Platform Hotkey Library
articles.description: An alpha release of a universal keyboard interaction toolkit that tackles all the rough edges and gotchas of different environments. Focused on React for now, but agnostic at its core.
articles.author: TanStack LLC

articles.title: fetch-network-simulator: Intercept fetch to Simulate Poor Network Conditions
articles.description: At its heart is a library that intercepts fetch and applies rules to do things like drop random requests, delay them, or slow them down, so you can see how resilient your app is.
articles.author: Karn Pratap Singh

articles.title: Vercel Introduces 'Geist Pixel'
articles.description: Vercel commissioned a variety of fonts for its Geist design system, with the latest addition being an aliased/bitmap variant.
articles.author: Vercel
